items.title: The 'hunting season' came for Iranians, then the US struck

children.content: As we journey into the heart of Iran, we meet the women who support the clerics and want revenge on the man who killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

image.altText: A woman holds a rifle as other women look on.

gallery.altText: Women in traditional Islamic attire with their hands raised in mourning.

thumbnail.altText: Women in traditional Islamic attire with their hands raised in mourning.

avatar.alt: A woman in a portrait taken against a deep red background.

items.title: Time for Russia to make a deal to end Ukraine war, Trump says

children.content: Donald Trump says Moscow should "make a deal" to end the war with Ukraine as G7 leaders have agreed to intensify pressure on Russia.

image.altText: Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Emmanuel Macron smiling.

items.title: Jacinta Allan refuses to admit cost of CFMEU corruption to taxpayers

children.content: Embattled Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan has refused to answer repeated questions over how much CFMEU corruption has cost taxpayers in the state, a scandal that has put the government and her leadership under threat.

image.altText: A woman wearing glasses holds a document while speaking to reporters in a conference room.

items.title: El Niño declared by BOM and it could become the strongest on record

children.content: El Niño is a state which can linger for up to 12 months and disrupt weather patterns across the globe, with Australia especially prone.

gallery.altText: A graphic of the Earth with the majority of areas highlighted in yellow and orange indicating warmer weather around Aus.
A graphic with oceans around Australia highlighted in yellow, orange and red to indicate higher temps.
A graphic of Australia showing higher chances of above median rain in WA, which is much lower in others states.

thumbnail.altText: A graphic of the Earth with the majority of areas highlighted in yellow and orange indicating warmer weather around Aus.
A graphic with oceans around Australia highlighted in yellow, orange and red to indicate higher temps.
A graphic of Australia showing higher chances of above median rain in WA, which is much lower in others states.

avatar.alt: ABC meteorologist Tom Saunders smiling in profile photo

items.title: Deal says 'loud and clear' that Iran won't have a nuclear weapon: Trump

children.content: Speaking on the sidelines of the G7 in France, Donald Trump says Iran will never have nuclear weapons.

image.altText: Donald Trump, wearing a blue suit and red tie, points.

items.title: Russian artist who satirised Putin shot dead in Poland

children.content: Two Belarusian nationals are arrested in connection with the killing, authorities say.

image.altText: A man wearing a hat at a demonstration on a street.

children.content: The Reserve Bank keeps the interest rate at 4.35pc as Australia's economy slows down.

items.title: Two men arrested by Dezi Freeman investigators

children.content: Police executed seven warrants at properties across Victoria and New South Wales this morning.

image.altText: A screen grab of a man looking seriously at a the camera.

items.title: FBI says plot to attack White House UFC event with drones thwarted

children.content: US media says five people are in custody in connection with the plot, that involved flying explosive-laden drones towards buildings near last weekend's White House UFC match.

image.altText: A canopy over a UFC ring in the dark sky with the White House in the background

items.title: The war has been a disaster for Trump and his aims in Iran are unmet

children.content: The ceasefire agreement fits a pattern from Donald Trump where the signing of a deal appears to be the major goal and the details often seem a distant secondary consideration.

articles.title: Trump says Strait of Hormuz will be 'completely open' by Friday. Then what?

children.content: The months-long shutdown of the the crucial shipping route has rocked global oil markets and flowed through to all sorts of prices. How will the reopening change things, and how fast?

image.altText: Petrol prices

articles.title: 'We'll never do this again': Why the UFC won't return to the White House

children.content: Despite touting the massive success of the White House event, UFC boss Dana White says the company will never stage another event like it.

image.altText: Josh Hokit puts a necklace on US president Donald Trump after a UFC fight at the White House.

articles.title: The ceasefire deal shows one country's influence and it isn't the US or Iran

children.content: Chinese, Russian and Iranian officials met about a day before the deal was publicly announced, with experts saying it proves Beijing and Moscow helped shape the agreement.

image.altText: Two men shake hands in front of Chinese and Russian flags

gallery.altText: Donald Trump sits next to a US flag.

thumbnail.altText: Donald Trump sits next to a US flag.

items.title: Birth doula says lack of consent delayed Triple Zero call for dying mother

children.content: A court probing the death of Stacey Warnecke after a freebirth has heard that emergency care was delayed for several minutes because her "birth keeper" would only call an ambulance if she consented.

image.altText: Stacey Warnecke smiles at the camera.

items.title: Craft retailer Lincraft to close rest of its stores in Australia and NZ

children.content: Retailer Lincraft will close the remainder of its shopfronts after more than 80 years of providing fabric, materials and homewares to Aussie crafters.

image.altText: A large white-on-blue 'Lincraft' sign hangs over a storefront, with the tag line 'where ideas come to life'.

items.title: Quick hits: Cabo Verde finds player on LinkedIn, meet Merlin the Duck

children.content: Cabo Verde's incredible result against Spain brings with it no shortage of amazing stories, while a duck in a jersey is stealing hearts in Mexico. Here are today's quick hits from the World Cup.

articles.title: Tunisia names new World Cup coach after sudden axing

children.content: Tunisia wastes no time in appointing a new coach at the World Cup following the sacking of Sabri Lamouchi in the wake of the humiliating 5-1 loss to Sweden.

image.altText: Hervé Renard looks on before the 2025 CONCACAF Gold Cup quarterfinal match between Mexico and Saudi Arabia in 2025.

articles.title: Iran's players celebrated at World Cup but complicated feelings remain

children.content: After a challenging, politically charged build-up to their World Cup campaign Iran's 2-2 draw with New Zealand brought mixed feelings for the Iranian diaspora.

image.altText: Iran player Ramin Rezaeian smiles and holds arms outstretched after scoring a goal

articles.title: Australian ref explains gesture after World Cup monitor calls for sacking

children.content: The Australian referee at the centre of a controversy around an alleged hate symbol being used during the World Cup broadcast has responded.

image.altText: Shaun Evans at the World Cup in referee uniform

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith's bail conditions varied to attend war memorial event

children.content: War veteran Ben Roberts-Smith's bail conditions have been varied so he can attend an opening at the Australian War Memorial in Canberra next week.

image.altText: Ben Roberts-Smith exiting Downing Centre Sydney, smiling, in a navy suit.

items.title: Teenager accused of planning terror attack targeted LNP over nuclear policy

children.content: A teenager accused of planning a terror attack in Brisbane allegedly wanted to target the Liberal National Party over their nuclear policy.

image.altText: Peter Dutton

items.title: My parents were much older than those of my peers — it made life difficult

children.content: Having older parents has shaped my life in many ways, including taking on a carer role for Mum and Dad in my 20s.

items.title: 38yo man jailed for life over 'highly brutal murder' of 22yo wife in NT

children.content: Braden Jentian, 38, has been sentenced to life behind bars for the "protracted and highly brutal murder" of his 22-year-old female partner, whose body was found in a vacant block in Katherine in 2024.

image.altText: A white, modernist, flat-fronted building with "supreme court" in capital letters above a wide entrance.

items.title: Officer tells inquest he gave man one second to drop knife before fatal shot

children.content: A Special Emergency Response Team officer tells a coronial inquest it took one second or less to storm a bathroom and fire the shot that killed Far North Queensland man Aubrey Donahue during a hostage siege.

image.altText: A man looks at the camera

items.title: Ex-Hawke minister-turned Catholic priest farewelled in Hobart

children.content: Michael Tate — an academic, politician, diplomat and Catholic priest — has been remembered as a generous man whose life was one of "warmth and service to others".

gallery.altText: Michael Tate wears a black suit, black shirt and priest's collar and stands outside the University of Tasmania law faculty.

thumbnail.altText: Michael Tate wears a black suit, black shirt and priest's collar and stands outside the University of Tasmania law faculty.

items.title: Rabbits are everywhere in the wild, so why is rabbit meat so expensive?

children.content: Once a cheap meat for families doing it tough, the price and availability of rabbit means it has gone from family dinner to fine dining.

image.altText: A rabbit in a paddock.

items.title: Five positive things having cash in the bank can do for you

children.content: Saving is not the only tool you need to achieve financial security these days, but it still has a role to play if you can manage it.

image.altText: Two hands hold a piggy bank full of coins.

children.content: A US Air Force B-52 bomber, designed to carry nuclear and conventional weapons, crashes shortly after taking off at an air base.

items.title: Taylor visits Alice Springs, renews call for Indigenous funding audit

children.content: Federal Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has visited Alice Springs, where he has met family of Kumanjayi Little Baby and renewed the Coalition's calls for deeper scrutiny of federal funding directed to the region.

items.title: Sydney shark attack victim loses arm ahead of 'long' recovery, family says

children.content: The family of Sydney mother Leah Stewart, who was mauled by a shark at Coogee Beach, says she has more surgeries scheduled for coming days.

articles.title: 'Highly migratory': Shark cull won't improve beach safety, expert says

children.content: Culling sharks would not reduce the risk of attacks, a marine biologist says, as the family of Sydney shark attack victim Leah Stewart says she has a "long road to recovery" ahead of her.

items.title: Alice Springs Victims of Crimes branch to close amid funding insecurity

children.content: The Alice Springs branch of Victims of Crime NT will be closed by the end of the week, with the organisation's chief executive citing funding insecurity. The closure will mean victims in the region will only be able to access services remotely.

image.altText: Man with gray moustache / beard, serious expression, sitting in an office, wearing a navy blue polo shirt.

items.title: Sydney's oldest trains suspended after teens break into cabin and use horn

children.content: They have operated on the city's network for almost 45 years, but were abruptly withdrawn from service this week.

image.altText: A picture of an old K set train at a station platform.

items.title: Mum describes 'gut-wrenching' moment baby taken in car theft

children.content: The mother of a baby taken as part of a car theft says more action needs to be taken to keep streets safe, as police continue their investigations.

image.altText: A woman with black hair holding a baby.

items.title: 91yo's 'best mate' saved from house fire by quick-thinking police officer

children.content: A fast-acting police officer has saved a feisty Chihuahua from a burning home in the South Burnett.

gallery.altText: A man with his face blurred holding a small dog.

items.title: 'Reasonable possibility' father injected insulin himself, murder trial jury told

children.content: A woman standing trial accused of murdering her parents with insulin has denied the allegations, while her defence lawyer has told the jury the possibility one of the alleged victims injected a fatal dose of insulin himself cannot be ruled out.

image.altText: A woman with shoulder length hair and sunglasses.

items.title: Canberra man faces trial for attempting to murder his wife after night out

children.content: A jury hears a Canberra man on trial for allegedly attempting to murder his wife whispered "I'm going to kill you" in her ear shortly before stabbing her in the arm with a kitchen knife.

image.altText: The exterior of a concrete building that reads "Law Courts of the Australian Capital Territory" over the entrance.

items.title: Mother of murdered Broome man slams WA Police culture

children.content: Ingrid Bishop says poor culture within WA Police hampered the investigation into her son Josh Warneke's death from the start.

image.altText: A woman in a floral shirt speaks to reporters outside court

items.title: NSW Ombudsman slams 24/7 prisoner lock-in as 'unlawful and oppressive'

children.content: The NSW Ombudsman says 33 protective custody prisoners at South Coast Correctional Centre were unlawfully locked in their cells 24/7 for up to three months.

image.altText: A sign saying South Coast Correctional Centre

items.title: Artemis III pilot Luca Parmitano recalls witnessing bushfires from space

children.content: Luca Parmitano was commanding the International Space Station when bushfires devastated eastern Australia. He has a blunt warning for the future of the planet.
